rulings.Case Title: J.R. MARKETING, LLC., A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al VS. HARTFORD CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 16, 2011, Line 1, DEFENDANT HARTFORD CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT - Summary adjudication granted as to all four of the issues. Notwithstanding the enormous quantity of paper filed by the parties, each issue, at its core, presents a question of law as to which there is no dispute regarding the material facts. As to issues one and three, Hartford has no duty to post the appeal bond or defend the Nevada action because, as a matter of law, the claims in the Marin interpleader action and the Nevada action are neither covered nor potentially covered by the Hartford policies. As to issue two, as a matter of conflicts law, the law of Virginia applies and thus Hartford has no duty to pay for independent counsel in the Virginia action. As to issue four, as a matter of law, Mr. DeMartinis is neither a manager nor an employee, as those terms are used in the JRMLLC policy, and thus Hartford has no duty to defend him. The hearing will be at 1:30pm, not 9:30am. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hek

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES LI VS. DEMAS YAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Dismissing The X-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 16, 2011, Line 5, PLAINTIFF CHARLES LI's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Dismissing The Cross-Complaint is Granted without leave to amend. A client has an absolute right to discharge counsel and control the litigation. (Fracasse v. Brent (1972) 6 Cal.3d 784, 790; Blanton v. Womancare, Inc. (1985) 38 Cal.3d 396, 404). Cross-complainant does not provide any basis to suggest that this basic deficiency in cross-claims can be cured. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: KIM KREIS et al VS. AMERICAN MULTI-CINEMA INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Discovry Adn Monetary Sanctions;
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 16, 2011, Line 2, PLAINTIFFs KIM KREIS, KATRINA KREIS' Motion For Discovery And Monetary Sanctions. Granted in part and denied in part. Granted as to form interrogatory 12.3. The representation in the opposition memorandum that defendants have no responsive information should be in the response. Granted as to form interrogatory 15.1. Defendants need to provide a substantive response as to each defense. Granted as to special interrogatory 2. Defendants need to provide Tsang¿s employer and contact information. Granted as to special interrogatory 10. Defendants need to provide a substantive response and/or prepare a privilege log. Granted as to special interrogatores 21, 22, and 23 and document requests 9, 14, and 15. The discovery relates to the issue whether defendants had actual or constructive notice of a defect on their property. The responses may be limited to the past 5 years. It is not unduly broad or burdensome since the parties agree that only 39 facilities are implicated. Denied as moot as to document request 19. Defendants represent that the insurance policies were provided. All further responses due by January 5, 2012. Defendants to pay $2,440 (8 hours at $300 per hour plus $40 in costs) to plaintiff by January 30, 2012. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MATT NAWROCKI VS. PAUL BISAILLON DBA ESTHETE MANAGEMENT
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 16, 2011, Line 11, DEFENDANT PAUL BISAILLON DBA ESTHETE MANAGEMENT DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Overruled. Where a contract is ambiguous, ¿a general demurrer to the complaint admits not only the contents of the instrument but also any pleaded meaning to which the instrument is reasonably susceptible.¿ (Aragon Haas v. Family Security Ins. Services Inc. (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 232, 239). The contract here is ambiguous and plaintiff has alleged a reasonably susceptible interpretation. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: DOROTHY SMITH VS. CHARLES DUNN COMPANY INC., et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 16, 2011, Line 13, DEFENDANT CHARLES DUNN REAL ESTATE SERVICES, INC., (SUED AS DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Overruled as to first, second, tenth, eleventh, twelfth, thirteenth and fourteenth causes of action, sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the fifth, sixth, seventh and eighth causes of action, and sustained without leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action. The amended complaint sufficiently alleges that defendant purposefully attempted to drive plaintiff from the premises without just cause after she complained of habitability defects. Plaintiff has leave to plead a contractual relationship or a third party beneficiary theory of recovery. The fourth purported cause of action does not state a viable independent claim. rulings.Case Title: TYRONE L. ADAMS VS. CHARLES L. EASLEY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Requesting Coordination Of Cases
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 16, 2011. Line 19. PLAINTIFF TYRONE ADAMS' Notice Of Motion And Motion Requesting Coordination Of Cases. Denied. Mr. Adams fails to show that coordination is more efficient than stay of all but one of the actions he filed pending the conclusion of one action or that, even if coordination is appropriate, the coordinated cases should be in San Francisco county rather than Sutty County where the property is located and which may be closer to his residence than is San Francisco. rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER TAKTANI et al VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 16, 2011. Line 20. MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL AS ATTORNEY OF RECORD FOR PLAINTIFFS CHRISTOPHER TAKTANI AND ALICIA HAPAI-TAKITANI. Denied without prejudice to being refiled upon a proper showing. The motion fails to provide good cause for withdrawing as counsel. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 16, 2011. Line 21. DEFENDANT FRANKLIN SQUARE HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Granted with ten days leave to amend. rulings.Case Title: DANIEL BALSAM et al VS. MONKER PRIVACY SERVICES LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 16, 2011. Line 24. PLAINTIFFS DANIEL BALSAM, CATHY RILEY, KRISTINA KIRBY AND ANGELA BRIDGES' DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled as to the first affirmative defense, sustained with leave to amend as to the second and third affirmative defenses and sustained without leave to amend as to the fourth and fifth affirmative defenses. The first affirmative defense is standard pleading. The second and third affirmative defenses should identify the ¿third parties¿ and the ¿applicable laws.¿ The fourth and fifth purported affirmative defenses are not actually affirmative defenses. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)
